title: Benchmark Contamination
concept: Contamination occurs when benchmark test questions leak into the model's pretraining data, inflating evaluation scores artificially.

exercises.prompt: What does it mean when we say an AI benchmark is 'contaminated'?

options.text: Test questions leak into training data
The AI gets a computer virus
Users give bad reviews to the AI

exercises.explain: Contamination happens when the test questions are leaked into the dataset used to train the AI.

cards.title: The Mirage of Smart AI
cards.body: Have you ever met someone who is great at passing specific tests but struggles with basic real-world tasks? The same thing happens to AI models when they 'cheat' by seeing the test questions early!

cards.title: What is Benchmark Contamination?
cards.body: Benchmark contamination happens when test questions used to evaluate an AI are accidentally mixed into the data used to train the AI. This causes the AI to artificially score high on tests without actually being that smart.

cards.title: The Exam Leak
cards.body: Imagine a teacher gives you a practice test, but accidentally leaves the exact same questions on the real exam. You score 100%, not because you understand the subject, but because you already memorized the exact answers beforehand. That is benchmark contamination!

cards.title: Cheating the Code Test
cards.body: A team trains an AI on millions of web pages, including a public coding website. That website hosted the exact questions for the 'HumanEval' coding test. When tested, the AI gets a perfect score because it already saw the answers during training!

cards.title: High Scores Don't Mean Smart
cards.body: Many think a high benchmark score means an AI is incredibly capable in the real world. In reality, if the benchmark is contaminated, the high score is just an illusion created by memorized test questions.

cards.title: Visualizing the Leak
cards.body: Look at how clean data collection differs from contaminated data collection:

left.text: Training data and test questions are kept completely separate. The test measures real learning.

right.text: Test questions leak into the training data. The test only measures memorization.

exercises.prompt: What effect does benchmark contamination have on an AI's evaluation score?

options.text: It means the AI has achieved human-like consciousness.
It inflates the score artificially through memorization.
It makes the AI run much faster on cheaper hardware.
It helps the AI learn new languages automatically.

exercises.explain: A high score caused by contamination is artificial because the model just memorized the specific answers rather than learning the concepts.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Benchmark contamination makes it difficult to measure the true capability of an AI model.
exercises.explain: Contamination makes the AI look smarter than it actually is on tests, hiding its true performance limitations.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of benchmark contamination.
exercises.sentence: Contamination happens when test questions leak into the model's pretraining data.
exercises.explain: If the test questions are part of the pretraining data, the model will memorize them, causing artificial test success.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI behaviors spots a victim of benchmark contamination?

options.text: An AI that refuses to answer questions due to safety filters.
An AI that gets 100% on a benchmark but fails the simplest real-world versions of those tasks.
An AI that runs out of memory while processing a large document.

exercises.explain: The AI claiming to be a genius based on tests it already saw the answers to is a classic sign of benchmark contamination. The others are normal AI behavior or generic hardware errors.

exercises.prompt: You are auditing a new AI model that scored a perfect 100% on a college-level math exam, but fails at basic division when chatting with users. What is the most likely cause of this issue?

options.text: When a user asks too many hard questions at once
When test questions leak into the model's training data
When a model's computer server gets a physical virus
When we use AI to write benchmark tests for humans

exercises.explain: Benchmark contamination happens during training (pretraining). If the test questions are mixed in with the training data, the model memorizes them and looks artificially smart on tests while struggling in the real world.
